title: Cheapest Time to Book Umrah Flights from Pakistan — Month-by-Month Guide (2026)
description: When are Umrah flights from Pakistan cheapest? Month-by-month PKR price guide for Karachi, Lahore, Islamabad to Jeddah and Madinah. Saudia vs PIA vs flynas.
keywords: cheapest month for umrah from karachi
intro:
Umrah flights from Pakistan are among the most price-sensitive routes in the country — fares can swing from PKR 55,000 to PKR 180,000+ depending entirely on when you book and which month you travel. Ramadan and Hajj season push prices to their peak, while the shoulder months between November and February offer the best value for pilgrims who have flexibility.

This guide uses real pricing data from FlightRate's daily scraper to show you exactly which months are cheapest for Umrah flights from Karachi, Lahore, Islamabad, Multan, and Peshawar — and which airlines (Saudia, PIA, flynas) offer the best fares in each season.

sections.h2: Umrah Flight Prices from Pakistan — Month by Month
sections.content:
The Pakistan–Jeddah and Pakistan–Madinah routes follow a predictable seasonal pricing pattern. January to mid-February: lowest fares, PKR 55,000–80,000 from major cities. This is the sweet spot for value Umrah travel. Late February to March (Ramadan approaches): prices begin rising, PKR 75,000–120,000.

Ramadan month: peak pricing. Karachi–Jeddah can hit PKR 150,000–200,000 in the final two weeks of Ramadan as everyone tries to perform Umrah before Eid. Post-Ramadan (Shawwal): prices drop sharply for 4–6 weeks — excellent value at PKR 60,000–90,000. May to August: moderate pricing PKR 70,000–110,000, quiet Umrah season.

Hajj season (Dhul Hijja): non-Hajj Umrah pilgrims are restricted from Makkah during this period anyway, so flights are not the concern. September to November: prices moderate at PKR 65,000–100,000. December: slight rise due to winter holidays.

sections.h2: Cheapest Airlines for Umrah Flights from Pakistan — Saudia vs PIA vs flynas
sections.content:
Three main airlines serve Pakistan–Saudi routes (Jeddah and Madinah): Saudia, PIA, and flynas. Each has a different pricing model.

Saudia is the most consistent — direct flights from KHI, LHE, ISB to both JED and MED. 23 kg baggage economy. Typically mid-range pricing. Early booking discounts can make Saudia competitive with budget options. flynas is Saudi Arabia's budget carrier — often 15–25% cheaper than Saudia on the same route, but slightly less frequent. 20 kg baggage. No meals included. PIA is the Pakistani national carrier — pricing fluctuates widely. Can be cheapest or most expensive depending on the day. 23 kg baggage, meals included.

For pure price, check flynas first, then Saudia, then PIA. FlightRate compares all three daily so you see which is cheapest today.

sections.h2: Karachi to Jeddah vs Karachi to Madinah — Which is Cheaper?
sections.content:
Jeddah (JED) flights are generally PKR 5,000–15,000 cheaper than Madinah (MED) flights from Pakistan on the same dates. This is because JED has more airlines and higher frequency — more competition = lower prices. Pilgrims who want to start Umrah in Makkah fly into Jeddah (King Abdulaziz Airport), which is 80 km from Makkah.

Madinah (Prince Mohammad bin Abdulaziz Airport) flights are direct with fewer airlines. Some Umrah packages include a Jeddah arrival and Madinah departure (or vice versa). In this case, buy the Jeddah inbound ticket separately for better value.

From smaller Pakistani cities (Multan, Peshawar, Sialkot), Jeddah flights are available with Saudia and PIA. Madinah flights from these cities are fewer — Multan has some PIA and Saudia MED flights but less frequently than KHI/LHE/ISB.

sections.h2: How Far Ahead to Book Umrah Flights from Pakistan
sections.content:
For Ramadan Umrah (highest demand): book 3–5 months ahead. Last-minute Ramadan fares from Pakistan regularly exceed PKR 180,000 one-way. Many pilgrims book in October–November for the following year's Ramadan. For regular shoulder-season Umrah: 4–8 weeks ahead is optimal. You'll find the best price-availability balance. Less than 2 weeks before travel: fares spike 30–60% on all airlines.

Saudia and flynas both have flash sales — subscribe to their alerts or check FlightRate daily. Some of the best PKR Umrah fares appear 6–8 weeks out when airlines release discounted inventory. Never pay airport or last-minute prices for Umrah — with flexible dates, you can always find a better deal.

sections.h2: Umrah Flights from Multan and Peshawar — Options and Prices
sections.content:
Pilgrims from Punjab (Multan, Faisalabad, Sialkot) and KPK (Peshawar) have direct Umrah flight options, though fewer than Karachi, Lahore, or Islamabad.

Multan (MUX): Saudia and PIA fly direct to Jeddah and Madinah from Multan — particularly busy in Umrah season. Prices are similar to Lahore, sometimes PKR 5,000–10,000 more due to lower frequency. Peshawar (PEW): Saudia and PIA serve Jeddah and Madinah from Peshawar. Strong demand from KPK pilgrims. Frequency is lower than ISB — consider flying from Islamabad (1–2 hours drive) for more options and potentially better prices.

Faisalabad (LYP) and Sialkot (SKT): have some Saudi connections but limited. Passengers often find it worth driving to Lahore for more choices and lower fares.

sections.h2: Umrah Visa and Travel Tips for Pakistani Pilgrims
sections.content:
Pakistani nationals require an Umrah visa (not tourist visa) to perform Umrah. The visa is typically arranged through a licensed Umrah tour operator or directly via Nusuk (Saudi official platform at nusuk.sa). Cost: approximately PKR 15,000–25,000 for the visa processing through an agent.

Visa-on-arrival is NOT available for Umrah — apply at least 3–4 weeks before travel. Many pilgrims arrange Umrah packages (flight + hotel + visa) through Pakistani tour operators — compare the package total against buying components separately. Sometimes packages save money, sometimes they don't. Makkah hotel prices vary enormously by distance to Haram — budget for PKR 8,000–25,000 per night per room depending on category and proximity.

faq.q: What is the cheapest month to fly for Umrah from Pakistan?
faq.a: January and early February consistently offer the cheapest Umrah flights from Pakistan, with Karachi to Jeddah fares as low as PKR 55,000–70,000 one-way. The 4–6 weeks immediately after Ramadan (Shawwal) are also excellent value. Avoid Ramadan itself — fares can reach PKR 150,000–200,000.

faq.q: Which airline is cheapest for Umrah flights from Karachi?
faq.a: flynas is often the cheapest option for Karachi to Jeddah, typically 15–25% below Saudia on the same dates. PIA can sometimes be cheapest with promotional fares. FlightRate compares all three airlines daily in PKR — check current prices before booking.

faq.q: How much does a Karachi to Madinah flight cost in PKR?
faq.a: Karachi to Madinah (MED) flights range from PKR 65,000 to PKR 130,000 depending on season and airline. January–February offers the lowest fares. Ramadan and peak Umrah periods push prices to PKR 120,000–180,000+. Jeddah flights are typically PKR 5,000–15,000 cheaper than Madinah.

faq.q: Is Saudia better than PIA for Umrah flights?
faq.a: Both Saudia and PIA fly direct to Jeddah and Madinah from Pakistan. Saudia is generally more punctual and consistent. PIA fares vary widely — sometimes cheapest, sometimes most expensive. For reliability, Saudia is preferred. For price, compare both via FlightRate on your travel dates.

faq.q: Can I book Umrah flights without a package from Pakistan?
faq.a: Yes — you can book flights separately and arrange your own Umrah visa via Nusuk (nusuk.sa) or a licensed operator. Booking independently often costs less than all-inclusive packages if you're experienced with Umrah travel. FlightRate helps you find the cheapest individual flight fare in PKR.
